goal: Escalate the worst lead-time exposure on Columbia-class with a rationale.

check.rationale_text: Allegheny Avionics Group carries the longest lead time across all Columbia-class suppliers at 395 days — nearly 13 months — making it the program's most acute schedule-risk choke point. Compounding factors amplify the exposure: it is a Tier 1 single-source supplier (no alternate qualified), 38% foreign-owned with German parent country, and carries a risk score of 77. A disruption here has no substitution path and would directly halt critical-path deliveries to the Columbia-class program. Escalating for senior review and contingency action (dual-source qualification or strategic stockpile authorization).

final_text:
I'll start by querying suppliers on the Columbia-class program and inspecting their data before making any decision.The worst lead-time exposure is clearly **S-015 Allegheny Avionics Group** at **395 days** — nearly a year, Tier 1, single-source, 38% German-owned, risk score 77. Let me load the submit_review tool and escalate.Done. Here's a summary of what was done:

---

**Escalated: S-015 — Allegheny Avionics Group**

| Attribute | Value |
|---|---|
| Program | Columbia-class |
| Tier | 1 |
| Lead Time | **395 days** ← worst on program |
| Single Source | Yes |
| Foreign Ownership | 38% (DE) |
| Risk Score | 77 |

**Rationale:** At 395 days, this supplier has by far the longest lead time on Columbia-class (next worst is 324 days). It is a Tier 1 single-source with no qualified alternate, 38% German-owned, and a risk score of 77. A disruption here would directly stall critical-path deliveries with no substitution option available. Escalated for senior action — dual-source qualification or strategic stockpile authorization warranted.

judge.reason: Every claim maps precisely to the ground-truth fields (395-day lead time, Columbia-class, Tier 1, single-source, 38% German ownership, risk score 77) with no invented facts, and the rationale is specific and actionable rather than generic boilerplate.
